text: Fill in the blanks about skin and fascia.
questions: The two main types of skin are *thick* skin (found on palms and soles) and *thin* skin (covering the rest of the body).
A *dermatome* is the area of skin supplied by a single spinal nerve.
The skin weighs about *3.5:in kilograms* kg and covers approximately *2:in square metres* square metres.
Surgical incisions made along *Langer's:lines* lines heal with minimal scarring because they follow the orientation of collagen fibres in the dermis.
The superficial fascia (also called *subcutaneous* tissue) contains fat, cutaneous nerves, and superficial blood vessels.

overallFeedback.feedback: Keep studying — review the material and try again.
